coaching
intelligence
platform

A managed service platform that turns a phone or webcam into a biomechanical analysis tool. Live pose estimation, session intelligence, athlete tracking, and coaching insights - served from a local machine with zero client installation.

from a phone on the sideline
to coaching intelligence that sticks.

Scroll
about perform.
built by a practitioner.

Perform was designed by a broadcast engineer and competitive national para-athlete. That dual perspective shaped everything: a platform built by someone who understands both what coaches need on the sideline and what athletes experience on the field.

The problem it solves is simple. Meaningful biomechanical analysis has always required expensive hardware, calibrated laboratory setups, or proprietary wearables. Perform removes all of that. A phone, a QR code, and a browser is all the coach needs.

The engine underneath Perform is a proprietary broadcast graphics system already deployed in live sporting environments. It was built to parse any structured data source and render broadcast-quality overlays in real time. Computer vision is the new data source. The rendering pipeline does not change.

who it is designed for
Soccer development coaches working with youth academies, club programs, and individual athlete development. The platform is built for the coach who wants data-driven feedback without a data science background.
why it exists
Catapult, STATSports, and similar platforms cost tens of thousands per season and require wearables on every athlete. Perform delivers comparable coaching insight from a phone camera with zero hardware cost to the club.
how it is delivered
As a fully managed service. The operator provides and maintains the hardware. The client opens a browser. All infrastructure, updates, and data management sit behind the service boundary. No installation, no maintenance, no technical knowledge required from the coach.
the data advantage
Built on a source-agnostic schema from day one. Catapult, STATSports, or any future wearable integrates without structural changes. The platform is designed to grow alongside the client relationship, not require a replacement.
how it works
Athlete metrics - height, weight, and leg inseam - are combined with plyometric pose framing from the computer vision engine. That combined data feeds the graphics engine, which renders the analysis directly to the coaching dashboard in real time.

deployment options
two deployment models
⬡
On-Site Hardware
Edge deployment - operator provided
A compact SFF machine and GL.iNet OpenWrt router are provided and managed by the operator. The SFF runs the full Perform stack locally. The router provides the local network. Coach and camera operator connect to that network. Everything runs without internet dependency once configured.
SFF machine runs pose pipeline, Flask API, SQLite, and dashboard
GL.iNet router acts as access point and network edge, not the brain
Coach opens a browser. Camera operator scans a QR code
Full session data stays on-site, client-owned
Works without internet after initial setup
Operator manages hardware and pushes updates remotely
⬢
Server-Side Remote
Cloud-accessible - same stack, different location
The same Python stack deployed on a remote server or VPS. Coach and camera operator access via browser over the internet. WebSocket and HTTP architecture means no changes to the codebase. Camera connects via the same aiortc WebRTC flow. Suitable for clients without on-site hardware or for multi-site management from a central location.
Same Python stack deployed on VPS or cloud instance
Camera connects via aiortc WebRTC over internet
Coach dashboard accessible from any browser
VPN option for secure access without exposing ports
Suitable for remote coaching or multi-site operations
Data stored on server, operator-managed backup policy
technical architecture
how it connects
Coach Browser port 8787
↕ WebSocket / HTTP
Flask API + Metric Engine port 8788
↕ Frame buffer
MediaPipe Pose Pipeline Python process
↕ aiortc WebRTC
Phone Camera (connect.html) QR code access
↕ LAN or VPN
SQLite Session Store local / remote
SFF machine (on-site model)
Debian/Linux compact server. Runs pose pipeline, Flask API, SQLite, dashboard HTTP server, and aiortc WebRTC endpoint. Acts as local brain and orchestration layer. Caches config if internet drops. GL.iNet router provides WiFi, connects to SFF on LAN, executes config pushed from the SFF.
Ball tracking layer (toggleable)
MobileNet-SSD via OpenCV DNN runs alongside pose pipeline when enabled. Apache 2.0, zero new dependencies, full frame rate on CPU. Kick events, possession, and ball receipt written to SQLite via source-agnostic schema. Zero overhead when off.
Confidence scoring layer
Three-layer deterministic pipeline: input quality, pose stability, metric reliability. Every metric carries a confidence score and explainability string. Governs display tier, PR eligibility, alert suppression, and session benchmarking. No ML model, fully rule-based.
Source-agnostic data schema
Source field on every metric row. MediaPipe, MobileNet-SSD, and future wearables (Catapult, STATSports) write to the same SQLite schema. Integrations are additive, not structural changes.
live demo
see it running
live coach dashboard
session review
What is the coach looking at?
The live dashboard shows real-time biomechanical data from a single side-on phone feed. Six metric groups update every 500ms. Skeleton overlay renders on the MJPEG stream. Alerts only appear when a threshold is breached. The coach sees a decision surface, not a data dump.
How does the camera connect?
Coach scans a QR code on the dashboard. A Perform-branded page opens on the phone. WebRTC negotiation happens directly with the Flask server via aiortc. No external relay, no VDO.Ninja, no third-party infrastructure. The phone streams, Perform receives.
What does the athlete see?
A token-based portal showing personal bests, plain-language progress charts, session history, and skeleton overlay video. No login. No app. Accessible from any browser via a link the coach shares.
development rollout
build timeline
COMPLETE
Phase 1 - Pose Pipeline
MediaPipe landmark extraction, hip velocity, knee angles, trunk lean, symmetry score, sprint detection writing rep timestamps to SQLite. Validated against real walking footage.
COMPLETE
Phase 2 - Coach Dashboard
Live coach dashboard on port 8787. Metric cards with green/amber/red states, rep history, session summary, metric selection panel. Inter font local. Brand colours throughout.
COMPLETE
Phase 3 - MJPEG Stream
Annotated skeleton overlay stream rendered live in dashboard. Frame buffer architecture separating capture from serving. Camera source control added.
COMPLETE
Phase 4 - Camera Connection
aiortc WebRTC replacing all external relay. Perform-branded QR connect flow. Video file upload and analysis mode. Source selector dropdown.
COMPLETE
Phase 5 - Confidence Scoring
Three-layer deterministic pipeline: input quality, pose stability, metric reliability. Device model inference. Confidence tiers governing display, PRs, and alert suppression.
COMPLETE
Phase 6 - Athlete Profile
Form score, readiness score, confidence-filtered trend charts, fatigue decay curve, movement signature, session quality heatmap, breakdown detection, athlete comparison.
IN BUILD
Phase 7 - UX Redesign
Broadcast-style live view redesign, session review with rep explorer and video sync, shared nav and CSS across all views. Coach translation paragraph.
IN BUILD
Phase 8 - Ball Tracking
MobileNet-SSD via OpenCV DNN. Toggleable with zero overhead when off. Kick events, possession, receipt. Biomechanical correlation at event time.
Coming Next
V3 - Athlete Portal
Token-based athlete access. Personal bests, plain-language progress charts, session history, skeleton overlay video per session. No login, no app install.
V3 - Parent Portal
Simplified read-only view. Plain English metric summaries. Progress framed as improvement percentages, not raw biomechanical values. Shareable link per athlete.
V3 - Post-Session Export
Auto-generated PDF report. Confidence-filtered metrics. Coach translation paragraph. Formatted for sharing with athletes, parents, or sporting directors.
V4 - Wearable Integration
Catapult and STATSports data via their APIs. Source field on every metric row means integration is additive, not a schema change. GPS speed correlated with pose-derived mechanics.
V4 - Second Camera Angle
Front-on camera adding hip rotation, shoulder symmetry, and goalkeeper-specific lateral dive mechanics. Separate VDO.Ninja or aiortc stream, separate MediaPipe process, same metric schema.
BUILD MODEL
Built solo using AI-assisted development. Each phase is a structured prompt delivered to Claude Code in Zed. Clean module separation means phases build on each other without rework. V3 and V4 pace to client revenue - only built when a paying client is waiting for it.
intellectual property
clean stack, zero fees
Component Licence Status
MediaPipe PoseApache 2.0Cleared
OpenCVApache 2.0Cleared
FlaskBSD 3-ClauseCleared
aiortcBSD 3-ClauseCleared
SQLitePublic DomainCleared
MobileNet-SSDApache 2.0Cleared
PyTorchBSD 3-ClauseCleared
NumPyBSD 3-ClauseCleared
Inter FontSIL OFL 1.1Cleared
OpenCap (Stanford)Stanford ResearchExcluded
YOLOv8 (Ultralytics)AGPL-3.0Excluded

No licence fees. No revenue sharing. No source disclosure to clients. All IP protected as trade secret under managed service delivery model.

Graphics engine
Proprietary broadcast overlay renderer. Pre-dates Perform, already deployed in live sporting broadcasts. Containerised via Docker, adapted to local deployment.
Pose-to-metrics normalisation layer
Original Python module translating MediaPipe landmarks to coaching metrics. Velocity, angles, symmetry, fatigue indicators, sprint detection algorithm.
Coaching intelligence layer
Confidence scoring, fatigue curve modelling, movement breakdown detection, readiness scoring, movement signature profiling. Original work throughout.
aiortc camera layer
Self-contained WebRTC ingestion served from Flask. Perform-branded connect page. No external signalling visible at any point to operator or coach.
Source-agnostic session schema
Source field on every metric row. Catapult and STATSports are additive integrations, not structural changes. Schema designed for third-party data from day one.

Perform is a computer vision-based performance analysis tool for coaching and athlete development. It is not a medical device, clinical motion capture system, or biomechanics laboratory.
